series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the history or development of an agency or programs within the agency that have historical significance; includes narratives, summaries, scrapbooks, photographs, oral histories, and other related records SEE ALSO: (below) Agency Policies

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: routine records documenting the history or development of an agency or programs within the agency SEE ALSO: (below) Agency Policies

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: records depicting agency structure
decision-making records documenting the creation of a new agency or agency mergers/splits
operational records documenting the creation of a new agency or agency mergers/splits, including internal reorganizations

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting transitions between administrations
information and statistics compiled and analyzed for research purposes or to support the agency management function
series_metadata.legal_citation: NOTE: If data contains confidential information, abide by relevant restrictions

series_metadata.series_description: lists of people and organizations to whom information is distributed by the agency
records that are used for tracking the existence and location of information within the agency
records documenting the final disposition of public records; includes destruction logs and transfer forms SEE ALSO: (above), Public Records Requests (PUBLIC RELATIONS) Indices and Inventories

records documenting the management of public records; includes approved retention and disposition schedules, file plans, and other related records SEE ALSO: (above), Public Records Requests (PUBLIC RELATIONS) Indices and Inventories
materials that have no regulatory authority for the recipient and are received from outside the agency or from other units within the agency SEE ALSO: Media File (PUBLIC RELATIONS)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: records intended to verify the receipt of information; includes certified mail receipts

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting accreditations and certifications received by the agency; includes applications, final reports, and other related records; also includes evaluations of the agency by outside entities SEE ALSO: Authorizing and Licensing (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: schedules of agency staff and events SEE ALSO: Authorizing and Licensing (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)

internal and external communications (including e-mail) to and from elected officials, appointed officials, and agency staff who are involved in decision-making, policy development, or other high-level planning for the agency SEE ALSO: Governing and Advisory Body Correspondence (GOVERNANCE) (continued on following page)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: internal and external communications (including e-mail) to and from agency employees whose correspondence has been identified for long-term retention for the purposes of institutional memory and planning; includes correspondence on behalf of an organizational unit SEE ALSO: Institutional Correspondence (EDUCATION), Executive Office (GOVERNANCE), General Legal Correspondence (LEGAL), E-mail Backups (INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY) NOTE: Excludes adjudicatory communications related to court proceedings in the Judicial Branch

tracking_data.comments: Records will be maintained permanently in office, during which time the records will be appraised for possible transfer to the Archives. ; Records will be maintained permanently in office, during which time the records will be appraised for possible transfer to the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: internal and external communications (including e-mail) to and from all other agency employees NOTE: Employees are responsible for identifying and retaining any e-mails that are necessary for audit or other long-term retention purposes SEE ALSO: Institutional Correspondence (EDUCATION), Executive Office (GOVERNANCE), General Legal Correspondence (LEGAL), E-mail Backups (INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY) NOTE: Excludes adjudicatory communications related to court proceedings in the Judicial Branch

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received/Sent PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: unsolicited correspondence received by the agency that requires no response and is not subject to Executive Order No. 12 (2013) SEE ALSO: Institutional Correspondence (EDUCATION), Executive Office (GOVERNANCE), General Legal Correspondence (LEGAL), E-mail Backups (INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY) NOTE: Excludes adjudicatory communications related to court proceedings in the Judicial Branch

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: internal records such as memoranda, newsletters, and bulletins that circulate information within the agency SEE ALSO: PUBLIC RELATIONS
routine notices, task lists, and arrangements SEE ALSO: Travel Requests (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)
minutes and attachments for internal committees or task forces SEE ALSO: Governing and Advisory Body Minutes (GOVERNANCE)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: agendas, meeting packets, visual aids, presentations, notes, and audio/visual recordings for internal committees or task forces SEE ALSO: Governing and Advisory Body Minutes (GOVERNANCE)
agendas, meeting packets, notes, and other related records from meetings attended by agency personnel SEE ALSO: Governing and Advisory Body Minutes (GOVERNANCE)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning memberships or registrations on behalf of the agency or agency personnel SEE ALSO: Accounts Payable (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)
records concerning organizations with which the Director of the State Bureau of Investigation has been affiliated SEE ALSO: Accounts Payable (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: documentation of procedures employed within the agency

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the design, planning, development, control, or monitoring of a specific project or group of projects; includes proposals, schematics, forecasts, feasibility studies, statements of work, assessments, and other related records SEE ALSO: Construction Management (INFRASTRUCTURE MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting special projects that establish new programs or services for the agency that become ongoing functions and priorities SEE ALSO: Construction Management (INFRASTRUCTURE MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: reports required to be submitted to the General Assembly SEE ALSO: Monitoring Surveys and Reports (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: reports of unmarked human burial and human skeletal remains submitted to the Department of Natural and Cultural Resources SEE ALSO: Monitoring Surveys and Reports (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)
other reports required to be submitted to the agency SEE ALSO: Monitoring Surveys and Reports (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)
annual and biennial reports SEE ALSO: Financial Reports (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: PERMANENT (archival) ∞-OR- Transfer to State Publications Clearinghouse, State Library of North Carolina: a) Report (printed): 2 print copies AND an electronic copy, or 10 print copies b) Report (no print run): 1 electronic copy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: monthly, bimonthly, quarterly, or semi-annual reports SEE ALSO: Financial Reports (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: daily or periodic activity reports concerning workload measurements, time studies, productivity, services rendered, or other similar records SEE ALSO: Financial Reports (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: agency policies that have state-wide significance SEE ALSO: HIPAA Policies (HEALTHCARE), Personnel Administrative Records (HUMAN RESOURCES), Electronic Records Policies (INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: plans for managing the agency’s units, projects, workforce, etc.; includes timelines, benchmarks, responsibilities, and routine work instructions

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Execution of plan PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: high level definitions of priorities for the agency
long-term plans intended to carry out the agency’s core functions

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the development of strategic plans

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Adoption of plan PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy
